channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Gravita India Profit Dips 3% As Revenue Jumps, Plans Gujarat Copper Plant
item.description: Gravita India reported a 3.4% year-on-year decline in net profit for the fourth quarter, reaching ₹92 crore. This contrasts with strong double-digit growth in revenue, up 13.1% to ₹1,173 crore, and a 22% jump in EBITDA to ₹113 crore. The company also revealed plans for a ₹160 crore copper recycling plant in Gujarat and the acquisition of Rashtriya Metal Industries Limited.

item.title: Aave Labs Adds Security Checks for Crypto Collateral After $300M Exploit
item.description: Following the Kelp DAO exploit that cost nearly $300 million, Aave Labs is fundamentally changing how it assesses collateral. New criteria will now scrutinize interoperability and cybersecurity alongside financial risk. This shift, spurred by the $293 million rsETH crisis, is also championed by the industry-wide 'DeFi United' initiative, aiming to restore confidence and demonstrating DeFi's growing capacity for self-rescue.

item.title: India's Ceramic Tiles Target Premium Design to Challenge Rivals
item.description: India's ceramic tile industry is rapidly upgrading, moving beyond low costs to focus on design, quality, and global appeal. Manufacturers in places like Morbi are investing in advanced production and curated collections to meet international demand for sophisticated looks. This shift means tougher competition with established European design leaders and requires a strong focus on sustainability to succeed in higher-value global markets.

item.title: Indian Firm LSI-MECH Engineers Joins ADNOC Middle East Oil Supply Chain
item.description: LSI-MECH Engineers has been prequalified by ADNOC, marking a significant entry for Indian manufacturers into the Middle East's vital oil and gas supply chain. This comes as global energy companies diversify suppliers due to geopolitical changes and major regional investments. While promising, the path requires meeting strict standards, proving reliable delivery, and navigating market risks and competition.

item.title: BPCL's $20B Mozambique LNG Project Faces Market Skepticism
item.description: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd. (BPCL) reports its Mozambique LNG venture is 42% complete. This $20 billion+ investment, operated by TotalEnergies, is progressing after the force majeure was lifted in November 2025. The project aims to support India's gas-based economy but faces market caution due to global energy volatility and analyst skepticism about its long-term valuation.

item.title: InMobi Acquires MobileAction for AI-Driven iOS Growth Amid Privacy Changes
item.description: InMobi has bought MobileAction, an AI platform for app analytics and Apple advertising. The acquisition aims to boost InMobi's services for the privacy-focused iOS market. By adding MobileAction's tools, InMobi will provide app developers and marketers with more accurate, AI-driven ways to grow their apps, keeping pace with industry changes.

item.title: Prudent Corporate Advisory Services Posts 14% Q4 Profit Jump, Revenue Up 27%
item.description: Prudent Corporate Advisory Services reported a 14% net profit increase in Q4 to ₹59 crore, driven by its mutual fund distribution and insurance businesses. Revenue climbed 27%, with quarterly average AUM in the MF segment rising 26% and insurance growing 41%. The company announced a ₹3.50 per share dividend, defying a negative Nifty 50 trend with significant equity AUM growth.

item.title: V-Mart Profit Down 39% Despite Sales Jump; Stock Edges Up
item.description: V-Mart Retail's fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026 saw net profit fall 39.1% year-on-year to ₹11.3 crore, despite a strong 24.5% revenue increase to ₹971 crore. Operational results were positive, with EBITDA surging 56% and margins expanding to 11%. The company proposed a 10% dividend, and its shares closed up 3.57% on May 7. The market seems to favor the company's focus on top-line growth and operational improvements over the dip in net profit.

item.title: Govt Updates State GDP Calculation with 2022-23 Base Year
item.description: India's Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) has established uniform guidelines for measuring states' Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP). The 2022-23 financial year is now the standard base year, replacing the earlier 2011-12. This change aims to boost accuracy, consistency, and comparability in tracking regional economic performance nationwide.

item.title: Mumbai-Pune Expressway Link Opens, Boosting Realty But Questions Remain
item.description: The 13-km Mumbai-Pune Expressway 'Missing Link' is now open, cutting travel times by up to 30 minutes and bypassing the difficult Bhor ghat. This upgrade is expected to boost real estate development and prices in Pune, Navi Mumbai, and Lonavala. However, the long-term success of this boom will depend on broader economic conditions, potential regional differences, and the financial health of major infrastructure projects.

item.title: Vedanta Shares Jump as Brokerages Target ₹1,000 on Demerger Hopes
item.description: Analysts are raising Vedanta's stock price target to ₹1,000, fueled by strong Q4 results and an upcoming demerger. They expect demerged units, set to list in June, to unlock significant value, pointing to improved fundamentals, cost savings, and strong cash flow.

item.title: Wonderla Posts Record Q4 Results, But Stock Dips on Valuation Concerns
item.description: Wonderla Holidays reported its best-ever fourth quarter, with net profit up 49% to ₹16.42 crore and revenue climbing 40.3% to ₹135.85 crore. The strong results were driven by its new Chennai park and Bengaluru resort, which boosted footfall by 25%. Despite the impressive figures, the stock closed slightly lower. This comes as consumers face spending pressures and the company trades at a high valuation, sparking questions about future growth.

item.title: India's LPG Costs Jump: Geopolitics Strains Pricing, Hits Consumers
item.description: India's plan to keep household LPG prices low is buckling under West Asian geopolitical tensions. While domestic cylinders are protected, commercial and smaller 'chhotu' (FTL) cylinders have seen sharp price increases. This forces Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) to absorb costs, straining finances and hurting informal workers and low-income families who rely on these smaller cylinders. The current subsidy system struggles with volatile global prices.

item.title: Dabur India Profit Soars 15% in Q4, Tackles Inflation with Price Hikes
item.description: Dabur India reported a 15% net profit increase to ₹369 crore in Q4FY26, fueled by strong domestic FMCG demand and 6% volume growth. To combat 10% inflation, partly driven by the West Asia conflict, the company is increasing prices by up to 4% and reducing grammage in smaller pack sizes. This strategy aims to offset rising input costs, especially for packaging, which has seen sharp price hikes due to elevated crude oil prices.

item.title: Ola Consumer's FY25 Revenue Plummets 42% Amid Fierce Competition
item.description: Ola Consumer's FY25 results show a sharp financial decline, with revenue falling 42% to ₹1,171 crore and operational losses doubling to ₹662 crore. This downturn stems from intense competition, particularly from Rapido, which has aggressively captured market share. Ola's standing in the four-wheeler segment is now estimated at 20-25%. Cost cuts haven't countered this pressure or Ola's shrinking cash reserves.

item.title: Dr. Reddy's, Nestle JV Launches Nutrition Aid for GLP-1 Therapy Patients
item.description: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories and Nestle Health Science have launched Celevida GLP+, a specialized nutrition product for patients in India taking GLP-1/GIP therapy. The product aims to fill nutritional gaps and help maintain muscle mass, supporting patients in the fast-growing diabetes and obesity market.

item.title: Motilal Oswal Launches Contra Fund to Capture Market Swings
item.description: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company has launched its 'Motilal Oswal Contra Fund' via a New Fund Offer (NFO) from May 8-22, 2026. The strategy targets fundamentally strong companies trading below intrinsic value, capitalizing on market dislocations and behavioral biases. This launch occurs amidst significant sectoral rotation and heightened equity market volatility, positioning the fund as a strategic bet on identifying value amidst uncertainty.

item.title: Dr. Reddy's-Nestlé JV Launches Celevida GLP+ to Support GLP-1 Patients
item.description: Dr. Reddy’s and Nestlé Health Science's joint venture has introduced Celevida GLP+, a specialized nutritional supplement in India for patients undergoing GLP-1/GIP therapies for type 2 diabetes and obesity. This launch strategically addresses protein deficits and muscle mass preservation, crucial concerns arising with increased GLP-1 drug accessibility post-semaglutide patent expiry. Celevida GLP+ aims to complement pharmacological treatments, bolstering Dr. Reddy's presence in the growing metabolic health sector with a recurring revenue stream.

item.title: Mindgrove-Pinetics Deal Puts India-Designed Chips Into Electronics Products
item.description: Mindgrove Technologies and Pinetics have signed a two-year commercial agreement to integrate India-designed semiconductor chips into finished electronic products. This collaboration aims to close India's long-standing gap between chip design and end-product manufacturing, allowing original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and original design manufacturers (ODMs) to access domestic chip solutions at the product level for the first time. The deal pairs Mindgrove's Secure IoT and Vision SoCs with Pinetics' product design and module development expertise for applications like biometric access, smart locks, and camera systems, strengthening India's semiconductor ecosystem.

item.title: Maharashtra Launches ₹500 Cr Plastic Recycling Network with Recove
item.description: Maharashtra is launching a 10-year, ₹500 crore initiative with Recove Ventures to build a large plastic recycling network. The partnership aims to fix a major shortage in recovering High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE) and Polypropylene (PP), which is currently below 30%, unlike PET at 95%. This is a response to new Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) rules demanding more recycled plastic and the financial risk of losing billions in valuable recyclables. The first project will be a ₹35 crore recycling plant in Additional Jalgaon MIDC, expected to start in late 2026.

item.title: Shree Cement's Volume Jumps 9% Yet Costs Squeeze Profit Margins
item.description: Shree Cement posted Q4 FY26 results with a 9% rise in volume to 10.8 million tonnes, but profitability suffered from margin compression. Despite steady sequential operating costs, EBITDA per tonne missed estimates by 5% at ₹1,161. The company forecasts cost increases of ₹150-200 per tonne in Q1 FY27. While capacity grew to 69 mtpa, investors are wary of rising inflation, favoring peers with stronger cost advantages. A small 2% target price adjustment reflects these mixed signals.

item.title: Moonshot AI Secures $2B, Hits $20B Valuation on Open-Source AI Surge
item.description: Moonshot AI has secured $2 billion in funding at a $20 billion valuation, driven by strong adoption of its open-weight Kimi large language models and over $200 million in annual recurring revenue. This valuation reflects intense investor interest in Chinese AI capabilities, placing Moonshot among a fast-growing group challenging global leaders.

item.title: Jobless Claims Tick Up Amid Surging Tech Layoffs
item.description: Initial jobless claims in the U.S. saw a slight increase to 200,000 for the week ending May 2, a figure that still landed below economist expectations. This uptick occurred while continuing claims dropped to a new two-year low of 1.77 million, underscoring a persistent "low-hire, low-fire" labor market dynamic. However, this overall stability masks a growing chasm, as the technology sector continues to announce escalating layoffs, reaching a three-year high for year-to-date cuts, even as broader private-sector announcements recede. The upcoming April jobs report is anticipated to show continued payroll growth, painting a picture of a dual-speed economy.

item.title: Thermax Beats Q4 Estimates Amid Valuation Concerns
item.description: Thermax Ltd. reported strong Q4 results, with net profit up 18.7% to ₹244.3 crore and revenue up 12.5% to ₹3,428 crore, both beating analyst forecasts. The order book grew 27% to ₹13,604 crore, boosted by a ₹1,600 crore contract. While the company proposed a ₹20 dividend, its high P/E ratio (around 67-73) compared to peers and analyst price targets suggesting a potential downside warrant investor attention.

item.title: CCL Products Stock Hits High Amid Revenue Surge as Margins Narrow
item.description: CCL Products India posted a strong fourth quarter with net profit up 12.4% to ₹115 crore and revenue surging 46.5% to ₹1,224.4 crore. Despite this, its EBITDA margin narrowed to 15.7% from 19.5% year-on-year. The company's stock, however, reached an all-time high of ₹1,202.75 on May 7th, signaling investor faith in its expansion.

item.title: India Extends Shipping Subsidies to FY31 to Boost Fleet
item.description: India's government has extended its subsidy program for domestic shipping firms flagging merchant vessels. The scheme, now running until FY31, aims to bolster the nation's maritime fleet and global presence. Approved with a Rs 1,624 crore outlay, it offers financial incentives on bids for government cargo imports.

item.title: IndiaAI and ICMR Unite to Build Health Data Ecosystem for AI
item.description: IndiaAI, the government's AI initiative, and the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) have partnered to build a strong AI system for healthcare. The collaboration integrates IndiaAI's tech with ICMR's research expertise to give innovators access to anonymized health data. The goal is to use AI to solve public health problems, speed up the creation of local health AI tools, and ensure strong privacy and ethical standards.

item.title: Westlife Foodworld Posts Strong Q4, But High Valuation Worries Investors
item.description: Westlife Foodworld, operator of McDonald's in West and South India, reported a strong fourth quarter for fiscal year 2026. Revenue grew 8.7% year-over-year to ₹655 crore, and net profit soared 55.92% to ₹2.37 crore. Operating EBITDA increased 12.7% to ₹86.85 crore, with margins improving to 13.25%. Despite these solid operational results, the company's stock has dropped over 28% in the past year, trading at a very high P/E ratio, suggesting a disconnect between its quarterly performance and market valuation.

item.title: BSE FY26 Profit Soars 88% to Rs 2,487 Cr, Board OKs Rs 10 Dividend
item.description: BSE Ltd's FY26 net profit soared 88% to Rs 2,487 crore on 63% revenue growth. The board recommended a Rs 10 dividend. BSE shares are trading at all-time highs amid strong market activity.

item.title: India Broker Complaints Per Investor Plummet Amid Investor Boom
item.description: Brokerage complaints in India have fallen sharply compared to the massive surge in active stock market investors over the past decade. Despite a 784% rise in clients on the NSE and 998% on the BSE, complaints per million investors have significantly decreased. Experts credit this to technological upgrades, electronic processing, and direct online trade execution, which have reduced operational issues.

item.title: Mumbai Water Metro Partners Hyke for Ferries, Faces Project Challenges
item.description: Maharashtra's Maritime Board has partnered with Norway's Hyke AS for the Mumbai Water Metro project. The deal focuses on supplying advanced, eco-friendly ferries and developing local shipbuilding. This aims to boost India's water transport potential, offering a sustainable alternative to crowded roads and rail. The project involves extensive terminal upgrades, but success hinges on managing operational and financial challenges, potentially setting a model for other cities.

item.title: Kraken Buys Payments Firm Reap for $600M, Boosts Asia Crypto Push
item.description: Kraken's parent company, Payward, is acquiring Hong Kong-based stablecoin payments firm Reap Technologies for $600 million in cash and stock. This marks Payward's first major infrastructure buy in Asia, aiming to expand Kraken's presence in the fast-growing crypto market there. The deal values Payward at $20 billion.

item.title: India Backs LICO With Grant to Boost Critical Minerals, Cut China Reliance
item.description: LICO Materials has received a ₹25 crore grant from India's Ministry of Mines under the National Critical Mineral Mission (NCMM). The funding will boost the domestic recovery of critical minerals like lithium, nickel, and cobalt. This initiative aims to strengthen India's self-reliance, cut import dependency (especially from China), and secure supply chains for clean energy and advanced manufacturing.

item.title: OnePlus India Shifts Online, Targets Premium Amid Smartphone Market Slowdown
item.description: OnePlus is consolidating its Indian operations, closing its Bengaluru office and relocating staff to Gurugram. This move, combined with a major shift to online-first sales and the closure of most partner stores, suggests a reduced market focus. The company plans to use Oppo's service network for efficiency, aiming for sharper pricing and innovation in the competitive premium smartphone market.

item.title: Tata Steel FY26 Results: India Surges, Europe Lags Ahead of Board Meeting
item.description: Tata Steel's board meets May 15, 2026, to approve FY26 results and consider dividends. Q4 showed strong 15% production growth and record deliveries in India, contrasting with flat or declining operations in Europe. Investors will focus on this India-Europe performance gap when assessing profit and shareholder returns.

item.title: Practo's AI 'Brain' Drives $100M GMV, Aims for Global Healthcare Impact
item.description: Practo hit over $100 million in gross merchandise value (GMV), driven by its advanced AI. The company is expanding AI engineering to create a central 'AI brain' for healthcare. This follows strong performance in its US market. Practo aims to use AI for better healthcare decisions worldwide. Recent financials show smaller losses and revenue growth, setting it up for future growth.
